req.title: Diagnostic report (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations; missing Observation links shall be rejected. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-037 requires: (1) a valid DiagnosticReport with at least one Observation reference is accepted (EP positive path); (2) a DiagnosticReport with an empty result array is rejected with 422 (NEG/DT negative path); (3) boundary timestamps (min epoch, max ISO-8601 date, extreme numeric valueQuantity) are correctly handled (BVA boundary path). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear-down their own Observation resources, ensuring idempotency. A state-transition case covers the lifecycle from 'registered' to 'final' status to verify the server enforces valid transitions.

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_with_valid_observation_reference_returns_201
preconditions: At least one Observation resource exists on the server
actions: POST /Observation with minimal valid payload to obtain an Observation ID
POST /DiagnosticReport with result array containing reference to the created Observation
Assert HTTP 201 and returned resource id is present
expected: Response body contains a non-empty id field
trace: R-HLT-037 – report stored when Observation references are present
EP: valid equivalence class – result array has >= 1 reference
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from tests.helpers import clinician_headers, base_url, create_observation, delete_resource

def test_diagnostic_report_with_valid_observation_reference_returns_201():
    obs_id = create_observation(clinician_headers())
    try:
        payload = {
            "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
            "status": "final",
            "code": {"text": "General Report"},
            "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
        }
        resp = requests.post(
            f"{base_url()}/DiagnosticReport",
            json=payload,
            headers=clinician_headers()
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 201
        body = resp.json()
        assert body.get("resourceType") == "DiagnosticReport"
        assert body.get("id")
    finally:
        delete_resource("Observation", obs_id, clinician_headers())

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_with_empty_result_returns_422
actions: POST /DiagnosticReport with result set to an empty array []
Assert HTTP 422 is returned
Assert response body contains an OperationOutcome or equivalent error detail
expected: Response body indicates missing Observation references
trace: NEG: invalid equivalence class – result array is empty
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from tests.helpers import clinician_headers, base_url

def test_diagnostic_report_with_empty_result_returns_422():
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "General Report"},
        "result": []
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url()}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "resourceType" in body or "error" in body or "issue" in body

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_boundary_timestamp_min_epoch
preconditions: At least one Observation resource exists on the server
actions: POST /Observation to obtain a valid reference
POST /DiagnosticReport with effectiveDateTime set to minimum valid ISO-8601 value '1970-01-01T00:00:00Z'
Assert HTTP 201 or 422 depending on server's stated minimum
Verify response reflects the timestamp correctly if accepted
expected: HTTP 201 with effectiveDateTime echoed as '1970-01-01T00:00:00Z', OR
HTTP 422 with clear message if server rejects epoch boundary
trace: R-HLT-037 – boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
BVA: lower boundary of effectiveDateTime – Unix epoch 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from tests.helpers import clinician_headers, base_url, create_observation, delete_resource

MIN_TIMESTAMP = "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z"

def test_diagnostic_report_boundary_timestamp_min_epoch():
    obs_id = create_observation(clinician_headers())
    try:
        payload = {
            "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
            "status": "final",
            "code": {"text": "Boundary TS Report"},
            "effectiveDateTime": MIN_TIMESTAMP,
            "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
        }
        resp = requests.post(
            f"{base_url()}/DiagnosticReport",
            json=payload,
            headers=clinician_headers()
        )
        assert resp.status_code in (201, 422)
        if resp.status_code == 201:
            body = resp.json()
            assert body.get("effectiveDateTime") == MIN_TIMESTAMP
    finally:
        delete_resource("Observation", obs_id, clinician_headers())

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_boundary_extreme_numeric_value_quantity
preconditions: Server is reachable at BASE_URL/Observation and BASE_URL/DiagnosticReport
actions: POST /Observation with valueQuantity.value set to 1e308 (near float64 max)
POST /DiagnosticReport referencing that Observation
Assert HTTP 201 or 422; if 201 verify value is preserved without overflow
expected: HTTP 201 with numeric value preserved, OR
HTTP 422 if value exceeds server-defined range with clear error body
trace: R-HLT-037 – boundary cases on numeric elements must be tested
BVA: upper boundary of numeric valueQuantity approaching float64 max
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from tests.helpers import clinician_headers, base_url, delete_resource

EXTREME_VALUE = 1e308

def test_diagnostic_report_boundary_extreme_numeric_value_quantity():
    obs_payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "Extreme Numeric"},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": EXTREME_VALUE, "unit": "mg/dL"}
    }
    obs_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url()}/Observation",
        json=obs_payload,
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert obs_resp.status_code in (201, 422)
    if obs_resp.status_code != 201:
        return
    obs_id = obs_resp.json()["id"]
    try:
        dr_payload = {
            "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
            "status": "final",
            "code": {"text": "Extreme Numeric Report"},
            "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
        }
        dr_resp = requests.post(
            f"{base_url()}/DiagnosticReport",
            json=dr_payload,
            headers=clinician_headers()
        )
        assert dr_resp.status_code in (201, 422)
    finally:
        delete_resource("Observation", obs_id, clinician_headers())

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_status_transition_registered_to_final
preconditions: Server supports PATCH or PUT for DiagnosticReport updates
actions: POST /Observation to obtain a valid reference
POST /DiagnosticReport with status=registered and the Observation reference
PUT or PATCH the created report changing status from registered to final
GET the report and verify status is final
expected: Initial POST returns HTTP 201 with status==registered
trace: R-HLT-037 – report stored; status lifecycle registered->final
STC: state transition from registered to final is valid workflow
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from tests.helpers import clinician_headers, base_url, create_observation, delete_resource

def test_diagnostic_report_status_transition_registered_to_final():
    obs_id = create_observation(clinician_headers())
    dr_id = None
    try:
        payload = {
            "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
            "status": "registered",
            "code": {"text": "Transition Report"},
            "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{obs_id}"}]
        }
        post_resp = requests.post(
            f"{base_url()}/DiagnosticReport",
            json=payload, headers=clinician_headers()
        )
        assert post_resp.status_code == 201
        dr_id = post_resp.json()["id"]
        updated = dict(payload, status="final", id=dr_id)
        put_resp = requests.put(
            f"{base_url()}/DiagnosticReport/{dr_id}",
            json=updated, headers=clinician_headers()
        )
        assert put_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
        get_resp = requests.get(
            f"{base_url()}/DiagnosticReport/{dr_id}",
            headers=clinician_headers()
        )
        assert get_resp.status_code == 200
        assert get_resp.json().get("status") == "final"
    finally:
        if dr_id:
            delete_resource("DiagnosticReport", dr_id, clinician_headers())
        delete_resource("Observation", obs_id, clinician_headers())

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_result_absent_field_returns_422
actions: POST /DiagnosticReport with the result field entirely omitted
Assert HTTP 422 is returned
Assert response body contains an actionable error message referencing result
expected: Error body references the missing result / Observation link
trace: R-HLT-037 – missing Observation links shall be rejected
DT: decision rule – result field absent maps to rejection outcome same as empty array
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from tests.helpers import clinician_headers, base_url

def test_diagnostic_report_result_absent_field_returns_422():
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "No Result Field"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url()}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers()
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    raw = str(body).lower()
    assert "result" in raw or "observation" in raw or "issue" in raw
